text:
Urban traffic congestion is one of the most serious global problems, causing a great loss for the economy and environment, as well as affecting the emergency rescue work. Most existing traffic management systems based on fixed-logic timing scheduling are incapable of dealing with the dynamic changes of the current road traffic status. Recently, the traffic status monitoring methods based on IoT and deep learning have been proposed. Although methods based on IoT have explored to learn traffic status by estimating the traffic density using deep learning algorithm, however, they can barely realize the emergency vehicles effectively due to the limited and low quality of the special designed images.

Intelligent Traffic Management using Multi-Modal Distributed IoT Framework: A New Approach This paper presents multi-modal distributed IoT framework for intelligent traffic management system, with two innovative contributions. Firstly, as the stationary part of the infrastructure does not change over time, we propose a new vehicle detection approach namely R-YOLO. R-YOLO utilizes a road-masking technique to minimize the search area, as a result it detects the vehicles with an accuracy of 77%, which is much higher than the existing state-of-the-art end-to-end deep learning-based approaches. The proposed approach maintains the low latency feature that is required in edge devices, and is proved to be deployed on low-cost hardware platforms such as Raspberry Pi. Secondly, the vision-based approach fails to identify emergency priority vehicles, so an acoustic approach with sliding window method is proposed to identify the sirens of emergency vehicles using their unique siren frequencies with an average detection accuracy of 99.4%. The proposed multi-modal distributed IoT system leverages vision and acoustic sensing to provide robust and low-cost real-time intelligent traffic control.
